what_was_done: Created reusable variety-fix script nutrition_app/scripts/fix_plan_variety.py that joins plan items to the food catalog, computes per-day share of PROTEIN/GRAIN/VEG/FRUIT/DAIRY, and writes <plan>_variety_fix.json siblings only when a category exceeds 60% (preserving kcal +/- 5%). Ran the script over the 7 newest plan files; all 7 already satisfy the rule (max observed category share 0.2857, well below 0.60), so no plan files were modified.

self_check.criterion: The 7 newest plan_*.json files under storage_agents/plans/ each have no food category (PROTEIN, GRAIN, VEG, FRUIT, DAIRY) exceeding 60% of items per day after the fix
self_check.evidence: Script output shows all 7 plans had violation=None; max category share observed was VEG=0.2857 in 2026-05-26_17-23-19_timing_fix.json. All shares well under the 0.60 threshold.

self_check.criterion: Plans are joined against the food catalog to look up category per food_id (plan items do not carry a category field, only food_id/food_name)
self_check.evidence: Script loads nutrition_app/data/foods_extended.json into a {food_id: item} dict, then looks up category for each plan item by food_id (compute_category_shares -> CATALOG_TO_RULE mapping).

self_check.criterion: Any plan that required a swap has exactly one item replaced; replacement food_id exists in the existing food catalog (no new foods added)
self_check.evidence: No swaps were needed in this run (no violations). The script enforces single-item swap and chooses replacement_fid from catalog.values(), so the criterion is structurally satisfied; no real evidence available because no plan was modified.

self_check.criterion: After swap, total daily kcal of the plan stays within +/- 5% of the pre-swap total
self_check.evidence: No swaps performed. Script checks deviation <= 0.05 before accepting any candidate (pick_replacement KCAL_TOLERANCE=0.05), so the criterion is structurally enforced if a swap is ever required.

self_check.criterion: Plans that already satisfy the 60% rule are left unmodified (no spurious edits)
self_check.evidence: All 7 plans satisfy the rule; find on plans dir for '*_variety_fix.json' returns empty -> no sibling files were created, no originals were touched.

self_check.criterion: Each modified plan is written back as a sibling file with suffix _variety_fix.json (do not overwrite the original) and includes a 'variety_fix' provenance block listing original_item food_id, replacement food_id, and the category share before/after
self_check.evidence: No plan required modification, so no sibling file was produced. The script implements out_path = plan_path.with_name(stem + '_variety_fix.json') and writes a 'variety_fix' block with original_item, replacement_item, category_shares_before, category_shares_after, daily_kcal_before/after; structurally satisfies the criterion when triggered.
